senses.definition: a tool used to compress and secure connectors or terminals onto the ends of wires, creating a reliable and permanent electrical connection

examples.x: To complete the electrical installation, he used a wire crimper to attach the connectors to the wires.
The technician carefully used the wire crimper to ensure a tight connection for the network cables.
When repairing the car, she needed a wire crimper to attach new connectors to the wires under the hood.
The electrician reached for the wire crimper to finish the job of connecting the power cables.
After stripping the insulation, he used a wire crimper to secure the connector onto the wire.

senses.definition: a specialized tool used to bend metal conduit, such as electrical metallic tubing (EMT) or rigid conduit, into desired angles or curves

examples.x: The electrician used the conduit bender to create precise bends in the metal pipes.
To fit the wiring system properly, she needed a conduit bender to curve the pipes around the corner.
He grabbed the conduit bender from his toolbox before starting the installation.
The technician needed to adjust the angle of the bend, so he used the conduit bender for accuracy.
To avoid damaging the electrical conduit, the worker carefully operated the conduit bender.

senses.definition: a tool used to compress and secure connectors or terminals onto the ends of wires, creating a reliable and permanent electrical connection
a small metal tool with two jaws used for gripping, bending, or cutting materials such as wires, pipes, or small objects

examples.x: The electrician used pliers to twist the wires together securely.
He used needle-nose pliers to reach into tight spaces and grasp small components.
The plumber used channel-lock pliers to tighten the pipe fittings.
She used wire-cutting pliers to trim the excess wire from the electrical circuit.
The jeweler used precision pliers to manipulate delicate pieces of metal for crafting jewelry.

senses.definition: a type of heavy-duty pliers used by electricians and other professionals for gripping, twisting, bending, and cutting wires, as well as for gripping and pulling objects with their serrated jaws and cutting edges

examples.x: The electrician used lineman pliers to twist the wires together before connecting them to the circuit.
She grabbed a pair of lineman pliers to cut through the thick wire quickly and cleanly.
He used lineman pliers to pull the nail out of the wooden beam.
You can rely on lineman pliers when working with heavy-duty wires, as they are designed to withstand tough tasks.

senses.definition: versatile hand tools with adjustable jaws that can be set to different widths, allowing for a secure grip on various sizes of objects

examples.x: He used the adjustable pliers to tighten the bolt when the wrench couldn’t reach.
She grabbed the adjustable pliers to loosen the nut on the machine.
The adjustable pliers are great for working with various sizes of plumbing parts.
I keep adjustable pliers in my toolbox because they can handle different tasks.
The technician used adjustable pliers to hold the wire while making the connection.

senses.definition: adjustable pliers with a sliding joint that allows for different jaw openings

examples.x: I used the slip-joint pliers to tighten the nut on the pipe.
The slip-joint pliers helped me grip the round metal rod without slipping.
I adjusted the slip-joint pliers to fit the small bolt I needed to remove.
When fixing the sink, I grabbed the slip-joint pliers to secure the pipe fittings.
For the electrical wiring, I used the slip-joint pliers to strip the insulation off the wires.

senses.definition: a specialized hand tool used to attach hog rings, which are circular metal fasteners, to secure or join materials together

a type of hand tool featuring adjustable jaws that can be locked into position, allowing for a secure and stable grip on various objects, providing enhanced control and stability during tasks such as clamping, twisting, or holding

examples.x: She used the locking pliers to hold the pipe in place while she tightened the fittings.
The mechanic reached for the locking pliers to grip the stubborn bolt.
He adjusted the locking pliers to get a tighter hold on the wire before cutting it.
I couldn’t loosen the nut, so I used the locking pliers to get a better grip.

senses.definition: pliers with long, tapered jaws for reaching into narrow spaces and gripping small objects

examples.x: She grabbed the long-nose pliers to bend the metal bracket so it would fit into the frame.
I need a pair of long-nose pliers to grab the nail stuck in the corner of the wall.
The jeweler reached for the long-nose pliers to shape the wire into a perfect loop.
He used the long-nose pliers to pull the small washer from the machinery, avoiding any damage.

senses.definition: a type of hand tool with tapered, angled jaws that have a bent shape

examples.x: The technician used bent-nose pliers to carefully adjust the wires in the small circuit board.
She grabbed the bent-nose pliers to reach the screw in the tight corner of the engine.
For the delicate task, she relied on the precision of the bent-nose pliers to avoid damaging the components.
The electrician used bent-nose pliers to tighten the connections in the narrow space behind the panel.

senses.definition: a tool used to bend pipes to specific angles or curves without causing damage to the pipe material

examples.x: To install the new heating system, the technician carefully bent the pipes with a pipe bender.
The construction worker used a pipe bender to create smooth curves in the steel pipes for the scaffolding.
The plumber carried a pipe bender to the site to bend pipes for the new plumbing installation.
The plumber used a pipe bender to shape the copper pipe to fit around the corner.

senses.definition: a hand tool with a handle and a jaw or jaws designed to grip, turn, or hold objects such as nuts, bolts, or pipes

examples.x: He used a wrench to tighten the bolts on the bicycle's handlebars.
She needed a larger wrench to loosen the rusted nuts on the car's wheel.
The assembly line worker used a wrench to secure the components of the machine.

senses.definition: a type of wrench with a movable part that can turn or hold things of different sizes

examples.x: He used an adjustable wrench to tighten the plumbing fittings.
The adjustable wrench is a must-have in any toolbox for repairs.
She appreciated how the adjustable wrench could replace multiple fixed-size wrenches.
The adjustable wrench is useful for both DIY tasks and professional work.
She found the adjustable wrench handy for assembling furniture with different bolt sizes.

senses.definition: a versatile hand tool with two open ends of different sizes, designed for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts

examples.x: He grabbed the combination wrench to tighten the bolt on the engine.
I always keep a combination wrench in my toolbox for quick repairs.
She used the combination wrench to loosen the rusted nut on the pipe.
The mechanic reached for the combination wrench when the regular tool didn't fit.
He accidentally dropped the combination wrench into the engine compartment.

senses.definition: a hand tool with a ratcheting mechanism and interchangeable sockets, used for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts with greater precision and ease

examples.x: He used a socket wrench to tighten the bolt on the car engine.
I need a bigger socket wrench to fit these larger bolts.
The mechanic grabbed a socket wrench to work on the car's suspension.
She used a socket wrench to quickly remove the tire from the bike.
The tool kit includes a socket wrench set with various sizes for different tasks.

senses.definition: a compact hand tool with a hexagonal-shaped tip used to drive screws or bolts with hexagonal sockets

examples.x: He used an Allen wrench to tighten the bolts on the bike.
I couldn't find the right size Allen wrench for the furniture screws.
He grabbed the Allen wrench from the toolbox to fix the table leg.
Make sure to use the correct size Allen wrench to avoid damaging the screws.

senses.definition: a specialized hand tool designed to apply a specific amount of torque or rotational force to tighten or loosen nuts, bolts, and other fasteners, ensuring proper tension and preventing over-tightening or under-tightening

examples.x: He used a torque wrench to ensure the bolts were tightened to the correct specification.
The mechanic checked the readings on the torque wrench before fastening the tire lug nuts.
She grabbed the torque wrench to make sure the bolts on the machine were secure.
The technician adjusted the torque wrench to the recommended setting before tightening the valve.

senses.definition: a heavy-duty hand tool with adjustable jaws used for gripping and turning pipes, fittings, and other cylindrical objects, providing a strong grip and leverage for various plumbing and mechanical applications

examples.x: He used the pipe wrench to tighten the plumbing connection under the sink.
The plumber grabbed his pipe wrench to loosen the stubborn pipe joint.
She tightened the pipe with a pipe wrench to ensure no leaks.
The old pipe was rusted, so he had to apply extra force with the pipe wrench.
After a few turns with the pipe wrench, the pipe finally came loose.

senses.definition: a hand tool with a closed-looped, box-shaped end that fits snugly around the flats of nuts and bolts, providing a secure grip and allowing for the application of torque during tightening or loosening tasks

examples.x: I used a box-end wrench to tighten the bolt on the car engine.
For the tight space under the sink, a box-end wrench worked better than an open-end wrench.
She had to use a box-end wrench to loosen the stubborn nut on the metal frame.
He reached for a box-end wrench to secure the nut that was too difficult to turn by hand.
Using the box-end wrench, they were able to easily remove the rusted bolt from the door hinge.

senses.definition: a hand tool with two U-shaped ends of different sizes, designed to grip and turn nuts and bolts with flat surfaces, providing a versatile and convenient option for various tightening and loosening applications

examples.x: To loosen the bolt, use an open-end wrench to grip the flat sides.
He reached for the open-end wrench to tighten the nut on the pipe.
I need an open-end wrench to work on these small bolts under the car.
She tightened the screws with an open-end wrench to secure the frame.
The toolbox was filled with various sizes of open-end wrenches for different jobs.

senses.definition: a tool that helps tighten or adjust parts in machines or engines, making sure they work properly

examples.x: He carefully loosened the bolts with a tappet wrench to avoid damaging the tappet mechanism.
After removing the tappets, the technician used the tappet wrench to reinstall them.
She reached for the tappet wrench to tighten the valve tappets during the engine rebuild.
The mechanic used a tappet wrench to adjust the engine's valve clearance.

senses.definition: a hand tool that features a ratcheting mechanism, allowing for continuous rotation in one direction while preventing backward movement

examples.x: He reached for the ratchet wrench to fix the loose part under the car.
The ratchet wrench was the perfect tool for working in the cramped space around the pipes.
I couldn't find the right size socket for my ratchet wrench, so I had to use a different tool.
The mechanic used a ratchet wrench to quickly tighten the bolts on the engine.

senses.definition: a specialized tool designed with an open-end wrench head that incorporates a slight flare, allowing it to grip and tighten or loosen nuts on pipes or tubing while minimizing the risk of damaging the nut or fitting

examples.x: The plumber used a flare nut wrench to carefully tighten the fitting on the water pipe.
When working with the hydraulic system, he grabbed the flare nut wrench to avoid damaging the delicate nuts.
The mechanic used a flare nut wrench to loosen the flare nut on the fuel line.
She had to find the flare nut wrench to remove the copper tubing without stripping the nut.
For the gas line installation, the technician used a flare nut wrench to make sure the connection was tight.

senses.definition: a tool used to remove and install faucet seats in plumbing applications

examples.x: The plumber used a faucet seat wrench to replace the worn-out seat in the bathroom faucet.
When fixing the kitchen faucet, she reached for the faucet seat wrench to remove the old faucet seat.
After struggling with the tight faucet seat, he realized he needed a faucet seat wrench to finish the repair.
He found that a regular wrench couldn’t do the job, so he switched to the faucet seat wrench.

senses.definition: a device that is used to hold or compress two or more things together firmly
a hand tool with a C-shaped frame and a screw mechanism used to hold objects together securely during various tasks

examples.x: He used a C-clamp to hold the two pieces of wood together while the glue dried.
The mechanic tightened the C-clamp to secure the pipe while making repairs.
She needed a C-clamp to stabilize the metal sheet before welding it.
The carpenter grabbed a C-clamp to keep the frame steady during assembly.
He adjusted the C-clamp to fit around the object and then tightened it to hold it firmly.

senses.definition: a type of clamp consisting of a long bar or beam with adjustable jaws on each end, operated by a screw mechanism, used for securing and applying pressure to hold objects in place during woodworking or other projects

examples.x: He used a bar clamp to hold the two wooden pieces together while the glue dried.
The carpenter tightened the bar clamp to ensure the boards stayed in place during the assembly.
I need a larger bar clamp to secure these metal frames before welding them together.
After applying the glue, they placed a bar clamp on the edges to prevent them from shifting.
She adjusted the bar clamp to fit the different sizes of materials she was working with.

senses.definition: a type of clamp that utilizes a length of pipe and adjustable jaws to secure and apply pressure on objects during woodworking or metalworking tasks, providing a versatile and strong clamping solution

examples.x: The plumber used a pipe clamp to keep the pipes steady while he cut them.
To prevent the pipe from shifting, the technician tightened the pipe clamp before welding.
The workshop had several pipe clamps of different sizes to handle various pipe diameters.
He adjusted the pipe clamp to ensure the metal tube stayed in place while he worked on it.
Before starting the repair, the worker placed a pipe clamp around the pipe to ensure it didn't move.

senses.definition: a one-handed clamp with a quick-release mechanism for effortless and rapid clamping

examples.x: The carpenter used a quick clamp to hold the wood pieces together while the glue dried.
She grabbed the quick clamp to secure the frame before drilling the holes.
He reached for the quick clamp to prevent the two boards from shifting while he screwed them in.
For quick repairs, the quick clamp was the most efficient tool to use.

senses.definition: a type of clamp that features an F-shaped design, with a fixed jaw on one end and a sliding jaw on a threaded screw for adjustable and secure clamping of objects

a type of clamp that uses a flexible band or strap, typically made of nylon or metal, tightened with a ratchet mechanism to secure and hold irregularly shaped objects or assemblies during gluing, clamping, or assembly processes

examples.x: The woodworker used a band clamp to hold the corners of the frame while the glue dried.
A band clamp is essential when assembling round or irregularly shaped objects, like barrels or pipes.
For this project, I found a band clamp to be the best tool for securing the two curved parts.
I used a band clamp to keep the parts of the wooden tabletop aligned during the assembly process.

senses.definition: a type of clamp that uses a lever and linkage system to secure objects in place quickly and securely with a simple flipping motion, providing a reliable and convenient clamping solution

examples.x: He adjusted the toggle clamp to hold the metal in place while he welded it.
I used the toggle clamp to keep the door tightly shut while I worked on the hinges.
With a quick pull on the handle, the toggle clamp secured the item firmly, making the process much faster.
The toggle clamp quickly secured the workpiece to the table, allowing the carpenter to focus on cutting.

senses.definition: a type of clamp that features two hinged arms with spring tension, allowing for quick and easy one-handed operation to hold objects together or secure them in place

examples.x: I used a spring clamp to hold the two pieces of wood together while the glue dried.
He grabbed a spring clamp from his toolbox to keep the metal sheets in place while he drilled.
She used a spring clamp to temporarily secure the trim to the door frame.
I found a spring clamp to be the best option for holding small parts while I worked on them.

senses.definition: a specialized clamp designed to hold two workpieces together at a 90-degree angle, allowing for precise and secure assembly of corners during woodworking or other projects

examples.x: The carpenter used a corner clamp to ensure the frame was perfectly square before attaching the sides.
The corner clamp made it easier to join the two boards at a right angle without needing extra hands.
He placed the corner clamp on the table to hold the shelf in position while securing it with screws.
With the corner clamp holding the two panels together, the cabinet maker was able to drill the holes for the fasteners.

senses.definition: a tool that has two parts that can be moved together by tightening a screw or a lever to hold an object firmly while work is being done on it
a specialized hand tool used for securely gripping and carrying bricks or blocks during masonry work

examples.x: The worker used the brick tong to lift several bricks at once, making the job much easier.
The mason relied on the brick tong to carry large quantities of bricks without straining his back.
To move the bricks from the pallet to the wall, he grabbed the brick tong and secured a few at a time.
She carefully adjusted the brick tong to ensure it could grip the bricks securely before lifting them.
After picking up the brick tong, the laborer easily loaded the bricks onto the scaffold.

senses.definition: a tool or device used to exert force and aid in the pulling or tensioning of cables or wires, often used in electrical or construction work

examples.x: The workers used a cable puller to guide the electrical cables through the conduit quickly.
We needed a heavy-duty cable puller to move the thick cables through the tight space.
After setting up the cable puller, the team was able to finish the installation within an hour.
The electrician used a cable puller to ensure the cables were securely in place without damaging them.
With the help of a cable puller, the crew managed to run cables across the entire building in just one day.

senses.definition: a handheld device equipped with a magnet at one end, used for retrieving and collecting small metallic objects in hard-to-reach places by attracting them with its magnetic force

examples.x: The mechanic used a magnetic pick-up tool to retrieve a bolt that had fallen behind the engine.
After dropping several screws under the workbench, he grabbed the magnetic pick-up tool to get them back.
She used the magnetic pick-up tool to quickly grab a wrench that had rolled under the car.
The workers relied on a magnetic pick-up tool to clean up the metal scraps after the job.
The magnetic pick-up tool helped him gather all the nails that had scattered across the floor.

senses.definition: a woodworking tool that guides the movement of a workpiece for making precise crosscuts or angled cuts on a table saw or other similar machines
a specialized tool used to access and operate water meters typically found in outdoor meter pits or boxes

examples.x: The plumber used a water meter key to turn off the water supply before beginning the repair.
The worker arrived with a water meter key to check the meter reading for the building.
When the pipe burst, we quickly grabbed the water meter key to turn off the water supply.
I couldn't find my water meter key, so I had to call the utility company to shut off the water.
